"What weeks yourself Italy?
Don't bored?
Lonely?"
have laugh when hear these questions, because nothing could further from truth.
might help describe "typical" here Lucca.
recent morning breakfast apartment then headed morning class Lucca Italian School (LIS), which starts 9:15.
next four hours were spent studying Italian with small class five students (one Zealander, Australian, Brazilian, from Virgin Islands, me).
class session Lucca Italian School with wonderful instructor Silvia.
Though different ages, nationalities, have somewhat varied linguistic skills, feel that lessons "fatto misura" (tailor made)
That this true because instructor, Silvia, perceptive, flexible, very, very talented.
have quickly coalesced into friendly supportive group today, over "pausa" (break) made plans lunch together next week.
After break couple hours lunch, returned school afternoon olive class with Antonella, both language instructor Maestrod'olio.
table olive tasting.
learned about history olive cultivation production Italy, especially Tuscany area around Lucca (Tuscany
olive producing region Italy, second only Puglia).
talked about methods olive production (both traditional modern), characteristics superior oil, well taste oils evaluate color, scent, flavor, uncover possible defects.
comparison tasting three oils (with apple slices cleanse palate between tastings) then sampled three typical Tuscan dish, accompanied local wines, course.
class informative, fun, social, practice Italian!
Although usually dinners home during week, which gives chance shop local markets experiment with preparing simple meals full local flavor, this night enjoyed dinner nearby restaurant Osteria Pasqualino Gubitosa (commonly known Pasquiale).
heard wonderful things about restaurant this first time eating there, joining another student from LIS.
wasn't typical Lucchese menu; chef owner both from southern Italy.
something new, pesce (fish) appetizer risotto were exquisite, company great.
Walking home, sound jazz band playing outdoor cafe followed wandered through quiet streets.
perfect ordinary, extraordinary here Lucca.
Bored?
chance!
